items.title: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
items.description: Discussion among Democrats have fixed on the president's personal gains when hashing out the crypto market structure bill's ethics provision.

items.title: Mizuho says Circle bank approval doesn't solve USDC growth, stablecoin competition risks
items.description: Japanese investment bank Mizuho reiterated its neutral rating on Circle, saying OCC approval for a national trust bank doesn't address slowing USDC growth or rising competition.

items.title: Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear
items.description: Analysts point to bitcoin’s resilience amid fresh U.S.-Iran escalation and renewed spot ETF inflows as the clearest signs yet that the marginal seller has stepped away.

items.title: Strategy pauses its Bitcoin buying spree to hoard a massive $3 billion cash cushion
items.description: Strategy now has more than 20 months of coverage for preferred-stock dividends and debt interest.

items.title: Robinhood built a blockchain for tokenized stocks. Memecoins took over
items.description: Robinhood Chain has pulled in $135 million of value and 800,000 addresses since July 1. Almost none of it is doing what the chain was built for.

items.title: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system
items.description: Crypto usage has spiked in the country, with transaction volumes hitting $430 million in the year after the central bank removed restrictions in mid-2024.

items.title: UK Treasury report on tokenization cites Ripple as convergence model
items.description: A Treasury-backed report targets putting repo, gilts and funds onchain within two years, and warms to permissionless networks that firms like Ripple have been building on.

items.title: Tom Lee's BitMine ether holdings rise to 5.77 million tokens, or 4.8% of total supply
items.description: The company expanded its ETH treasury while Chairman Tom Lee pointed to Robinhood Chain's rapid growth on Ethereum.

items.title: Bolivia mulls recognizing USDT as payment currency amid dollar shortage
items.description: As foreign currency reserves remain under pressure, Bolivia is considering a framework to let the world’s largest stablecoin be used for payments, savings and trade.

items.title: Hyundai completes USDT treasury settlement pilot between US and Mexico
items.description: Hyundai completed a proof-of-concept using Tether's USDT to settle a cross-border treasury transfer between its US and Mexican subsidiaries, highlighting growing enterprise interest in stablecoin-based payments.

items.title: Donald Trump invokes US senator’s death to push crypto bill
items.description: With the death of Senator Lindsey Graham and another senator hospitalized, Republicans’ current majority in the chamber has been reduced to 51-47, likely requiring more Democratic support to pass crypto market structure.

items.title: Bitcoin threatens $62K in risk-asset rout as Donald Trump says US will 'run' closed Hormuz Strait
items.description: Bitcoin bulls faced an uphill struggle to preserve $62,000 as stocks opened down on Strait of Hormuz rhetoric between the US and Iran.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Coinbase Ventures tops crypto VC list for H1 2026
items.description: Coinbase’s venture capital arm leads 2026 crypto investing even as funding withered and unique investors shied away amid the bear market.

items.title: Strategy sells $467M in MSTR shares, leaves 843,775 BTC stack untouched
items.description: Strategy boosted its US dollar reserve to $3 billion after selling $466.7 million in MSTR shares, while keeping its 843,775 Bitcoin holdings unchanged.

items.title: UK tokenization push could add as much as $44B to annual output by 2035: Report
items.description: The government-backed roadmap calls for the UK’s first digital gilt by early 2027 and seeks to make tokenized bonds usable for trading and borrowing.

items.title: Trump proposes 20% toll on cargo through Strait of Hormuz; restarts Iran blockade
items.description: The Strait of Hormuz is a major oil shipping route and the epicenter of the U.S. and Israel's ongoing war with Iran.

items.title: Paramount, WBD hit with lawsuit from 12 states, including California, to block merger
items.description: A group of states, including California, filed a lawsuit to block the merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery.

items.title: Trump recommends Lindsey Graham's sister serve as interim senator for South Carolina
items.description: The death of Sen. Lindsey Graham ignited a scramble among South Carolina Republicans to prepare for a special primary election to replace him on the GOP ticket.

items.title: Waller says Fed shouldn't 'fight the last war' on inflation but warns hikes still possible
items.description: The Fed governor said inflation has expanded beyond the often-cited drivers such as the energy price spike in tariffs.

items.title: Trump administration urges banks to scrutinize lending to immigrants without work authorization
items.description: Federal bank regulators issued guidance Monday seeking to curb mortgages, auto loans and other consumer credit to immigrants without U.S. work authorization.

items.title: SpaceX stock sinks for a second-straight day, nearing $135 IPO price
items.description: SpaceX went public a month ago in a record IPO. Elon Musk's space and AI company was added to the Nasdaq-100 last week.

items.title: Judge says Trump sued IRS for 'improper purpose'; refers his lawyer to bar
items.description: Trump's lawsuit against the IRS was settled by the Justice Department with an agreement to create a $1.8 billion "lawfare" fund that has since been scrapped.

items.title: A July rate hike from the Fed? The odds are rising
items.description: Chances of a rate hike in July by the Federal Reserve rose as oil prices jumped on the latest developments in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: US will charge 20% fee for ships using Strait of Hormuz, Trump says
items.description: President claims Washington will be ‘guardian’ of waterway and seek reimbursement to provide security for vessels

items.title: Jane Fraser’s ruthless remake of Citigroup
items.description: The bank has sold assets, cut jobs and made controversial new hires while cultivating a closer relationship with Trump. Is it enough to get back into the big time?

items.title: Trump’s star power becomes a gamble for Republicans
items.description: Republican Congress member in Pennsylvania’s Lehigh Valley faces voter dissatisfaction with Washington

items.title: Trump’s plan for Europe is falling apart
items.description: The US president has a genius for alienating existing and potential allies

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8IExFvX3LVo! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Global oil prices top $82 a barrel, heading for biggest jump in 3 months after Trump reimposes Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.description: The week is kicking off with fresh spikes for oil prices after President Donald Trump reimposed the naval blockage on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Inflation is primed to fall for the first time in 6 years. Will high prices drop too?
items.description: A surge in U.S. inflation to a three-year high has begun to recede, but life isn’t going to get more affordable for Americans anytime soon.

items.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
items.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

items.title: A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
items.description: There is a chance the Federal Reserve will need to raise interest rates soon if inflation readings this week disappoint, Fed governor Christopher Waller said Monday.

items.title: Billionaires are loading up on these superior stocks. Why it pays to follow their money.
items.description: The second quarter was a six-year best for U.S. stocks — here are the three sectors poised to lead the next leg higher.

items.title: Micron and other chip stocks feel the pain of imported volatility — blame SK Hynix
items.description: The SOX chip index was suffering a unanimous selloff, after SK Hynix’s stock had its worst day in 18 years in South Korea trading.

items.title: Can big banks’ ‘cheap’ stocks keep rising? We’ll find out when JPMorgan and an unusual number of others report earnings Tuesday.
items.description: Among the largest U.S. banks, Citigroup is expected to show the greatest improvement by one important measure. But it still has a long way to go to reach its own performance target.

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: Palantir: profits, procurement and power | FT Film

items.title: Shell sells Indian wind and solar business for $1.8bn
items.description: Aditya Birla Renewables takes on Shell’s renewable assets in India as energy major continues to refocus on oil and gas

items.title: What will Burnham do on immigration?
items.description: Likely next prime minister is looking to satisfy calls for tougher borders while appealing to Labour’s liberal supporters

items.title: Democratic states sue to block Paramount’s $110bn WBD acquisition
items.description: Lawsuit could delay deal and cost studio tens of millions of dollars

items.title: Yemen’s Houthis accuse Saudi Arabia of attacking international airport
items.description: Militant group threatens retaliation for assault apparently aiming to prevent Iranian aircraft from landing

items.title: Top Fed official warns ‘hot’ inflation could trigger rate rise
items.description: Christopher Waller says a further elevated reading may prompt central bank to tighten policy

items.title: Fees for seas: a history of taxing waterways
items.description: From Ottoman sultans to Danish kings, Iran’s toll system for Strait of Hormuz revives an old maritime fight

items.title: El Salvador’s Bukele set to run for third term
items.description: Authoritarian leader wins party primary unopposed

items.title: The deceptive promises of America’s democratic socialists
items.description: The way forward for the party is not to swing to the left

items.title: Trump Administration Is Snapping Up Stakes in Private Companies. Could A.I. Be Next?
items.description: Some tech executives are nervous that the administration’s recent scrutiny of artificial intelligence models could be a prelude to a demand for an ownership stake.

items.title: The Fed’s Christopher Waller Warns of Higher Rates Soon if Inflation Stays High
items.description: Christopher J. Waller, a governor at the Federal Reserve, said he needed to see several months of lower inflation data to feel confident about the outlook.

items.title: Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger Is Challenged in Court by California and 11 Other States
items.description: The lawsuit, filed by California and 11 other states, argues that the deal would harm movie theaters and damage America’s entertainment industry.

items.title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
items.description: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.

items.title: Will Oil Prices Rise as the US-Iran Truce Frays? The Answer Lies With China.
items.description: For decades, OPEC influenced the market by how much oil it produced. But China, the largest importer, is demonstrating its remarkable power over prices.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Traffic Falls to Lowest Point in a Month After Strikes
items.description: Shipping activity in the critical waterway has plunged after Iran attacked a vessel, setting off U.S. strikes. President Trump said he would reinstate a blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: Wall Street’s Big Week for Earnings and Economic Data
items.description: Earnings season kicks off in earnest on Tuesday with big banks up first. Corporate America faces a high bar to beat investor expectations.

items.title: Nearly 200 Economists and Tech Leaders Warn of A.I. Threats
items.description: A letter calls for policymakers to do more to understand and respond to potential disruptions from artificial intelligence.
